implementation_status.summary: Added an optional MoViNet attention-pooling replacement head that flattens the final feature map into tokens, applies learned query cross-attention pooling, and emits the existing binary violence logits. Wired the new head through train config, model construction, model loading, tests, and three 3866-derived TOMLs.
risks: Flattening the full late MoViNet feature map creates a larger token set than the prior MobileViT variant, so the head may still overfit or train noisily on the small finetune set despite using only a few learned queries.
A single learned query may be too restrictive if strong ordering depends on reading multiple distinct regions or moments, which is why the multi-query variant is included.

If the partially frozen A5 backbone expects the historical average-pool statistics, this replacement readout may still need a smaller capacity setting or additional regularization to match baseline ranking quality.

recommended_runs.reason: Primary high-conviction revision: one learned query changes only the pooling/readout behavior while keeping baseline training hyperparameters intact.
Secondary variant that lets the head pool from multiple discriminative regions before aggregation without changing the backbone or overall training setup.
Lower-capacity fallback to reduce overfitting risk if the default attention head is still too expressive for the partially frozen A5 finetune regime.
